RA09 – AI-enabled fraud, financial crime.description: AI used to increase the scale, realism, targeting or automation of fraud, scams, financial crime and exploitation.
what_counts: AI-enabled fraud, scams, phishing or impersonation
Voice cloning or synthetic media used for financial deception
AI-enabled money laundering, mule recruitment or synthetic identity fraud
Public, banking, payment, law-enforcement or regulatory responses
what_does_not_count: Generic business AI adoption with no crime or harm signal
General fintech automation with no fraud or exploitation relevance
RA09 – AI-enabled fraud, financial crime.query: (AI OR artificial intelligence OR generative AI OR deepfake OR voice cloning OR chatbot OR LLM OR synthetic media) (fraud OR scam OR phishing OR impersonation OR mule account OR money mule OR romance scam OR synthetic identity OR payment fraud OR financial crime OR money laundering)
RA09 – AI-enabled fraud, financial crime.semantic_profile: AI-enabled fraud, scams, phishing, voice cloning, impersonation, synthetic identity, payment fraud, money laundering and financial exploitation.

RA11 – Image-based abuse, VAWG and inter.description: AI-enabled image-based abuse, stalking, harassment, coercive behaviour and wider technology-facilitated interpersonal harms.
what_counts: AI-generated or AI-manipulated image-based abuse
AI-enabled stalking, harassment, coercion, extortion or impersonation in interpersonal abuse contexts
Platform, policing, victim support or regulatory responses
what_does_not_count: Generic deepfake entertainment stories with no victim, abuse, fraud, public safety or policy relevance
General online safety stories with no AI-enabled abuse signal
RA11 – Image-based abuse, VAWG and inter.query: (AI OR artificial intelligence OR generative AI OR deepfake OR synthetic media OR image generator OR voice cloning OR chatbot) (NCII OR image-based abuse OR intimate image abuse OR deepfake abuse OR VAWG OR stalking OR harassment OR coercive control OR technology-facilitated abuse OR digital abuse OR sextortion OR extortion)
RA11 – Image-based abuse, VAWG and inter.semantic_profile: AI-enabled image-based abuse, NCII, deepfake abuse, VAWG, stalking, harassment, coercive control, sextortion, extortion and technology-facilitated interpersonal abuse.

RA11 – Child safeguarding and online pro.description: AI-related child safeguarding, online child protection, platform safety, detection, reporting and prevention signals using public/professional terminology.
what_counts: AI-related child safeguarding and online child protection reporting
AI tools, platforms or model risks discussed by trusted safeguarding, regulator or law-enforcement sources
Detection, prevention, reporting, takedown or support responses
what_does_not_count: General education technology stories with no safeguarding or online protection signal
Generic parenting or online-safety stories with no AI signal
RA11 – Child safeguarding and online pro.query: (AI OR artificial intelligence OR generative AI OR deepfake OR image generator OR chatbot OR synthetic media) (child safety OR child safeguarding OR online child protection OR IWF OR NSPCC OR Ofcom online safety OR Thorn OR child protection technology OR safeguarding technology OR CSAM)
RA11 – Child safeguarding and online pro.semantic_profile: AI-related child safeguarding, online child protection, CSAM reporting, platform safety, detection, reporting, prevention, takedown and law-enforcement safeguarding signals.

RA13 – Terrorism, extremism and radicali.description: AI-enabled terrorism, extremist propaganda, radicalisation, recruitment, synthetic media and attack-planning capability signals.
what_counts: AI-enabled extremist propaganda, recruitment or radicalisation
Synthetic media, chatbots or model misuse connected to terrorism or violent extremism
Counter-terrorism, platform, law-enforcement or prevention responses
what_does_not_count: Generic political content or misinformation with no terrorism/extremism signal
General AI safety stories with no public safety or national security relevance
RA13 – Terrorism, extremism and radicali.query: (AI OR artificial intelligence OR generative AI OR chatbot OR LLM OR deepfake OR synthetic media) (terrorism OR terrorist OR extremism OR extremist OR radicalisation OR radicalization OR terrorist propaganda OR extremist recruitment OR attack planning OR violent extremism)
RA13 – Terrorism, extremism and radicali.semantic_profile: AI-enabled terrorism, terrorist propaganda, extremist recruitment, radicalisation, synthetic media, violent extremism and attack planning capability.

RA14 – Illegal item creation, acquisitio.description: AI-enabled facilitation of illegal item creation/acquisition, illicit markets and criminal capability uplift.
what_counts: AI tools or models facilitating illegal item creation or acquisition
AI-enabled illicit supply chains or marketplace facilitation
Policy, enforcement, platform or regulatory responses
what_does_not_count: General manufacturing, chemistry, 3D printing or logistics with no illicit or public safety signal
Generic AI capability stories with no crime or harm connection
RA14 – Illegal item creation, acquisitio.query: (AI OR artificial intelligence OR generative AI OR LLM OR chatbot OR AI agent) (illegal items OR weapons OR firearms OR drugs OR counterfeit goods OR dark web OR crimeware OR illicit marketplace OR 3D printing crime)
RA14 – Illegal item creation, acquisitio.semantic_profile: AI-enabled illegal item creation or acquisition, illicit markets, weapons, drugs, counterfeit goods, dark web facilitation and capability uplift.

Cybercrime, malware and model abuse.description: AI-enabled cybercrime, malware, ransomware, exploit development, phishing kits, jailbreaking, prompt injection and model/system abuse.
what_counts: AI-enabled cybercrime or automation of cyber offending
AI assistance in malware, ransomware, phishing, exploit development or vulnerability discovery
Prompt injection, jailbreaking, model tampering or misuse affecting safety/security
what_does_not_count: Generic enterprise cyber product marketing with no AI-enabled threat signal
Routine software vulnerability stories with no AI or model misuse connection
Cybercrime, malware and model abuse.query: (AI OR artificial intelligence OR generative AI OR LLM OR chatbot OR AI agent OR agentic AI) (malware OR ransomware OR phishing kit OR exploit OR vulnerability OR cybercrime OR jailbreak OR prompt injection OR model tampering OR data poisoning OR crimeware)
Cybercrime, malware and model abuse.semantic_profile: AI-enabled cybercrime, malware, ransomware, phishing kits, exploits, vulnerabilities, prompt injection, jailbreaking, model tampering, data poisoning and crimeware.

Realistic fakes, evidence, identity and.description: AI-generated realistic fakes affecting identity, public trust, evidential integrity, borders, immigration, courts and justice processes.
what_counts: AI-generated or AI-manipulated realistic fakes used in identity, evidence, immigration or justice contexts
Synthetic media affecting public trust, official processes or criminal justice outcomes
Detection, provenance, watermarking, verification or forensic response signals
what_does_not_count: Generic image/video generation with no identity, evidence, fraud, public trust or policy relevance
Creative AI stories with no harm, safety or institutional integrity signal
Realistic fakes, evidence, identity and.query: (AI OR artificial intelligence OR generative AI OR deepfake OR synthetic media OR voice cloning OR image generator OR video generator) (false evidence OR fake document OR fake identity OR identity abuse OR identity fraud OR border OR immigration OR court evidence OR misinformation OR impersonation OR provenance OR watermarking OR content authenticity)
Realistic fakes, evidence, identity and.semantic_profile: AI-generated realistic fakes, false evidence, fake documents, identity abuse, border and immigration risks, court evidence integrity, misinformation, impersonation, provenance and content authenticity.

Open-source and open-weight model risk.description: Risks and mitigations relating to open-source/open-weight model release, hosting, downstream modification, offline use and supply-chain control points.
what_counts: Open-source or open-weight model release, hosting, distribution or downstream modification
Evidence about offline use, fine-tuning, abliterated models, safeguard removal or model hosting controls
Supply-chain, regulatory, platform or international policy responses
what_does_not_count: General open-source software stories with no AI model or harm relevance
Generic AI openness debates with no risk, safeguard or policy signal
Open-source and open-weight model risk.query: (AI OR artificial intelligence OR generative AI OR LLM OR model weights OR open-weight OR open source model OR open-source model OR Hugging Face OR local model) (misuse OR harm OR safety OR safeguards OR fine-tuning OR jailbreak OR abliterated OR model hosting OR model release OR downstream modification OR offline use)
Open-source and open-weight model risk.semantic_profile: Open-source AI, open-weight models, model weights, local models, hosting platforms, downstream modification, fine-tuning, abliterated models, safeguard removal, offline use and supply-chain risk.

AI agents and autonomous capability upli.description: Emerging agentic systems, tool-use agents and autonomous workflows that may change offender capability, scale or operational response needs.
what_counts: AI agents, autonomous AI, computer-use agents or tool-use systems relevant to crime, public safety or national security
Evidence of agents improving task completion, automation, social engineering, cyber activity or fraud workflows
Safety, standards, monitoring or policy responses to agentic AI
what_does_not_count: Generic productivity agent announcements with no misuse, safety, governance or public sector relevance
Routine automation tools with no AI or autonomous capability signal
AI agents and autonomous capability upli.query: (AI agent OR agentic AI OR autonomous AI OR autonomous agent OR computer use agent OR browser agent OR tool use OR agentic workflow) (crime OR fraud OR scam OR cybercrime OR misuse OR safety OR security OR vulnerability OR autonomous attack OR prompt injection OR public safety)
AI agents and autonomous capability upli.semantic_profile: AI agents, agentic AI, autonomous agents, computer-use agents, browser agents, tool use, agentic workflows, criminal misuse, cybercrime, fraud automation, autonomous attack and public safety risk.

Use of AI to tackle crime and public saf.description: Examples of AI being used by law enforcement, regulators, platforms or public bodies to prevent, detect, investigate or respond to crime and harm.
what_counts: AI tools used to detect, prevent, investigate or respond to crime or public safety harms
AI used by police, regulators, platforms, financial institutions or public bodies in crime/safety contexts
Operational pilots, assurance, ethics, safeguards, governance or deployment evidence
what_does_not_count: Generic public sector AI productivity stories with no crime, public safety or harm relevance
Commercial AI product announcements with no operational, regulatory or public safety signal
Use of AI to tackle crime and public saf.query: (AI OR artificial intelligence OR machine learning OR generative AI OR algorithmic tool OR analytics) (police OR law enforcement OR regulator OR public safety OR crime detection OR fraud detection OR safeguarding tool OR investigation tool OR victim support OR content moderation OR digital forensics)
Use of AI to tackle crime and public saf.semantic_profile: Use of AI by police, law enforcement, regulators, platforms, financial institutions or public bodies to detect crime, prevent crime, support safeguarding, investigate harm, improve public safety or support victims.
